Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given two POWL models for a Hire to Retire process The standard process typically includes 1 Post a job advertisement Post Job Ad 2 Screen candidates Screen Candidates 3 Conduct interviews Conduct Interviews 4 Make a hiring decision Make Hiring Decision 5 Onboard the new employee Onboard Employee 6 Add the employee to the payroll system Add to Payroll 7 Close the case Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However note that the model is a partial order This means that activities that are not connected by a direct edge may be executed in parallel or in any order as long as the defined precedences are respected

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Post Job Ad is the first activity correct Screen Candidates comes after Post correct

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Screen to Decide meaning that after screening we can make a decision without interviewing That is an anomaly Also there is no edge from Interview to Decide

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The model does not force that the interview must be done before the decision Therefore the interview might occur after the decision That would be illogical

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The model allows Path 1 Post to Screen to Decide to onward without interview Path 2 Post to Screen to Interview but then what

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The model does not have any choice after the decision It forces Onboard to Payroll to Close So what if the decision is negative The model does not account for that

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 1 Missing constraint between Interview and Decide Lack of a clear sequence for interviews and decision No handling of negative decision

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The edge Post to Interview and Post to Screen means that after posting we can do either Screen or Interview in any order Or even in parallel This is not typical Normally we would expect to screen candidates first and then interview the shortlisted candidates

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The model does not enforce that screening must be done before interviewing It allows interviewing without screening That is an anomaly

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The loop on onboarding might be intended to allow multiple onboarding attempts But onboarding is typically a one time activity The loop might be an overcomplication and could lead to infinite loops

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The XOR choice for payroll means the payroll step can be skipped Why would we skip adding the employee to the payroll That is a critical step

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 2 Allows interviewing without screening or in parallel with screening The loop on onboarding is unnecessary The payroll step can be skipped Always positive decision forces onboarding after decision without considering negative outcome

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Both models have anomalies Let us compare Model 1 main issue is the missing constraint between Interview and Decide and the lack of a negative decision path Model 2 has more severe anomalies including skipping screening order skipping payroll and looping onboarding

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 despite its flaws at least includes the screening activity and forces that the decision comes after screening

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 has more severe anomalies It allows skipping payroll That is a critical business error The loop on onboarding is unnecessary and could cause problems

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Both models have the same flaw they do not handle negative decisions But Model 2 has additional flaws so Model 1 is better because it does not have these additional flaws

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 1 more closely aligns with a normative version of the Hire to Retire process
